key_insight: The paper verifies a sequential, non-iterative fixed-stress thermo-poroelastic coupling in PFLOTRAN that can reproduce benchmark pressure, temperature, and deformation responses.
engineering_relevance: It provides a validated THM modeling foundation for simulating subsurface energy systems such as enhanced geothermal reservoirs and thermal energy storage.
method: Solve TH mass and energy balance implicitly on control-volume/Voronoi cells.
Update quasi-static mechanics on an element-based dual mesh using a fixed-stress split.
Map fracture/discontinuity degrees of freedom between flow and mechanics representations.
evidence_signal: The implementation is assessed against poroelastic and thermo-poroelastic analytical or semi-analytical benchmarks, with agreement reported for pressure diffusion, temperature fields, and mechanical deformation.
watch_item: Because the coupling is strictly sequential and non-iterative, accuracy and stability may be sensitive in strongly coupled or highly discontinuous scenarios.
paper_title: Verification of a sequential thermo-poroelasticity formulation in PFLOTRAN
